opp.name: RUC_S2C_May 26'
opp.account: Regents of the University of California
opp.next_step: 6/17mc- Working internally to better understand RFP requirements and deadlines. Likely to engage with CPO shortly after that to introduce myself and assure aligment moving forward
opp.ais_why:
* RUC is an enterprise-wide S2C transformation across its campuses and healthcare entities, with scope spanning Spend Analytics, eSourcing, Savings Tracking, Intake & Orchestration and CLM.
* AI is in the evaluated scope: this enterprise S2C transformation explicitly includes "Intake & Orchestration" and CLM, with Salesforce noting "Strong alignment with Zycus' Spend Analysis, iSource, Merlin Intake, iContract, and Merlin AI capabilities."
* AI sits within the evaluated scope (Merlin Intake, Merlin AI) but as one element of a broad multi-module transformation rather than the stated driver; award is expected December 2026. Formal Evaluation, 20%.

Next_Step__c 17 Jun 2026: rep has not yet reviewed RFP requirements in depth

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case on record. Zero buyer discovery calls means Zycus has not yet learned what operational pain (cycle time, maverick spend, supplier consolidation, contract compliance risk) or financial target (cost savings %, headcount reduction, error-rate improvement) the UC system is solving for with this RFP. The RFP document itself likely names evaluation criteria and problem statements, but the rep has not yet reviewed it (per Next_Step 17 Jun: 'working internally to better understand RFP requirements'). Without a quantified value case Zycus cannot tailor the response to UC's priorities or differentiate on ROI. The move is to obtain the RFP immediately, extract the business objectives and success metrics, and if the RFP is vague on quantified goals, use the CPO engagement to surface them (e.g., 'What savings target or compliance improvement is the UC system expecting from the new S2C platform?').

sources: Zero OpportunityContactRole records; no Champion role identified
No Avoma calls (no one has advocated for Zycus on a call)
Zero buyer engagement in 28 days (no relationship exists to build on)
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Zero buyer engagement (no calls, no contact roles) means Zycus has no internal advocate in the UC system. RFP deals without a champion have sub-30% win rates: the champion shapes RFP criteria to favor Zycus, provides competitive intel (who else is bidding, what the eval committee values), coaches Zycus on UC system politics (which campuses hold sway, where resistance might come from), and shepherds the proposal through evaluation and approval gates. At day 28 with the RFP window potentially closing, the champion gap is acute. The move is to identify a potential champion in the first round of CPO/stakeholder engagement: look for a UCOP or campus procurement/finance leader who (a) owns the pain and the initiative, (b) has used or evaluated Zycus before (or has a peer who is a Zycus reference), (c) believes the UC system needs a modern AI-enabled S2C platform and sees Zycus as the best fit, and (d) has the credibility and access to influence the evaluation committee. Building the champion relationship is the top priority after re-engaging.

sources: Prior sweep memory: Jaggaer, Coupa, incumbent legacy (dated, no current confirmation)
No new Avoma calls, Salesforce Competitors__c field empty, no tasks or Next_Step mentions
Inference: higher-ed RFPs typically draw Jaggaer, Coupa, SAP Ariba, and sometimes Ivalua or GEP
competition.narrative: Competition partially known from prior sweep memory (Jaggaer, Coupa, incumbent homegrown/legacy), but no new evidence this sweep (zero buyer calls, no Salesforce competitor fields populated, no tasks/next-steps naming rivals). The prior read was: Jaggaer is a strong higher-ed S2C incumbent (common in university systems, particularly for eProcurement and contract management); Coupa is the scale/brand threat (cloud-native, strong ERP integrations, but often expensive for public-sector budgets); and UC campuses likely run fragmented homegrown or legacy systems (each campus may have a different stack) that the RFP aims to replace with a unified platform. However, without current intel (no RFP review, no buyer conversation, no knowledge of who else is bidding) Zycus cannot know which competitors are actually in the RFP shortlist, what their pricing or positioning is, or where Zycus has a wedge. The wedge hypothesis from prior memory is that Zycus can win on AI/Merlin differentiation (agentic negotiation, intake automation, spend analytics) and on fit for federated higher-ed procurement (campus autonomy with system-wide visibility), but this has not been validated with the buyer. The move is to confirm the competitive shortlist in the CPO call (e.g., 'Who else are you evaluating?') and to obtain the RFP Q&A log (which often reveals competitor questions and positioning).

sources: No buyer calls (Avoma returned zero meetings)

Next_Step__c 17 Jun: rep has not yet engaged buyer to discover pain
identify_pain.narrative: Pain unknown. Zero buyer discovery means Zycus has not learned what operational or financial pain the UC system is solving for. Typical higher-ed S2C pain points include: fragmented procurement across campuses (no spend visibility or supplier consolidation), manual contract workflows (slow cycle time, compliance risk, lost savings opportunities), legacy or homegrown systems that cannot scale or integrate with modern ERPs, lack of AI/analytics for category insights or supplier risk management, and maverick spend (departments bypassing procurement). The RFP's problem statement section will articulate some of this, but the real pain and its owner (which campus or UCOP leader is feeling it most acutely) are best learned in conversation. Without identified pain Zycus cannot position its solution as the pain-killer or build urgency. The move is to use the first CPO call to surface the pain directly (e.g., 'What's driving the UC system to invest in a new S2C platform now? What's broken or insufficient about the current state?') and to identify the pain owner (the leader whose budget, reputation, or strategic initiative depends on solving it).

sources: No buyer calls or contact roles covering legal, InfoSec, or IT integration
Next_Step__c 17 Jun: no contract or implementation discussion yet
Inference from UC public-entity status and higher-ed norms (SOC2, MSA, SOW, InfoSec gate)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting and implementation process unknown. UC system as a public entity will require an MSA (likely their standard terms for SaaS vendors), a detailed SOW covering implementation across 10 campuses, and an Order Form. Legal review will be thorough (public procurement regulations, data privacy, indemnification, limitation of liability, termination rights). InfoSec review is mandatory (SOC2, data residency, encryption, access controls; UC handles PII, financial data, and federally-regulated research data). ERP integration requirements (Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft at each campus) must be scoped in the SOW. Implementation will likely be phased by campus or module (e.g., pilot at one campus, then rollout). However, none of this has been discovered: no InfoSec contact, no IT/ERP integration stakeholder, no SOW discussion, no contract template shared. The RFP may specify contract terms and implementation expectations, but Zycus has not reviewed it. The move is to obtain the RFP's contract and implementation sections, identify the UC legal/InfoSec/IT contacts (likely listed in the RFP or available via the CPO), and confirm the paper path and any unusual UC system requirements (e.g., does each campus sign separately, or does UCOP sign on behalf of the system?).

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer unmapped. For a system-wide UC deal the EB is likely the UCOP (UC Office of the President) Chief Procurement Officer or VP Finance, who controls the budget for a shared-services S2C platform across the 10 campuses. No contact roles exist; no calls have identified who holds commercial authority. The prefetched account contact list includes Katie Smith (Procurement Leader, UCOP) and Charles Miskelley (Executive Director, Contracts, Business & Finance, UCOP) as potential UCOP-level budget owners, but neither is confirmed as involved in THIS RFP. Zycus has not yet engaged any UC stakeholder, so budget authority, approval chain, and whether individual campuses hold veto power over the spend are all unknown. The absence of an identified EB after 28 days is a critical MEDDPICC gap and a predictor of low win probability. The move is to map the EB in the first CPO call and confirm the approval process (UCOP-led vs campus consensus-required).

sources: No buyer engagement (zero calls, zero contact roles)

Stage Formal Evaluation implies RFP in-flight but no timeline data
decision_process.narrative: Decision process unknown. For a UC system-wide S2C RFP the process likely follows a standard higher-ed procurement path: RFP release → vendor Q&A period → proposal submission → evaluation committee scoring → shortlist (typically 2-3 vendors) → finalist demos/presentations → reference checks → negotiation → board or exec approval → contract. The evaluation committee likely includes UCOP procurement/finance leadership and campus CPOs or their delegates. However, Zycus has not yet engaged any stakeholder or obtained the RFP, so the submission deadline, evaluation timeline, committee composition, shortlist announcement date, and finalist demo schedule are all unknown. The Next_Step (17 Jun) suggests the rep is still preparing internally and has not yet initiated contact. The critical unknown is whether the RFP window is still open: if the submission deadline has passed, the deal is lost. The move is to contact the UCOP CPO or RFP coordinator immediately to confirm the RFP timeline and submission deadline, and if the window is still open, to request the full RFP document and Q&A log.

sources: No RFP review completed (Next_Step__c 17 Jun)
No buyer discovery calls to surface criteria

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria unknown. The RFP document will enumerate evaluation criteria (functionality fit, pricing, implementation timeline, references, vendor financial stability, InfoSec/compliance, ERP integration capability, AI/innovation roadmap), but Zycus has not yet obtained or reviewed the RFP (Next_Step 17 Jun: rep is 'working internally to better understand RFP requirements'). UC system-wide deals typically weight consensus across campuses, so criteria may include campus-specific fit (e.g., integration with each campus's ERP instance, support for federated vs centralized procurement models) and vendor ability to deliver a unified platform that accommodates campus autonomy. Without the RFP or a discovery call Zycus cannot know which criteria are weighted highest, whether AI/Merlin is a differentiator or table-stakes, or what the scoring rubric looks like. The move is to obtain the RFP immediately, map the stated criteria, and use the CPO engagement to learn the unstated priorities (e.g., 'Beyond the RFP scorecard, what will make one vendor the clear choice for the UC system?').

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI appetite unknown. No AIS score, no buyer calls, no discovery of whether UC system prioritizes AI/innovation in the RFP criteria. Higher-ed institutions are increasingly curious about AI for procurement (spend analytics, contract risk detection, supplier insights), but readiness varies widely. UC system as a large, multi-campus public entity may be 'AI Curious' (interested in innovation but cautious about implementation complexity and data privacy). Without the RFP or a buyer conversation, Zycus cannot tailor the Merlin positioning to UC's AI maturity level.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 29 May 2026 (28 days ago) and has been completely dark since: no buyer engagement, no calls, no emails logged, no contact roles added, no RFP obtained. Next_Step updated 17 Jun 2026 (9 days ago) by rep Rick Taranek noting he is 'working internally to better understand RFP requirements and deadlines' and intends to engage the CPO 'shortly after that to introduce myself and assure alignment moving forward.' This is a rep-side internal action, not buyer contact. Opportunity last modified 26 Jun 2026 (today), but LastActivityDate remains null (no logged buyer activity). No field history available showing stage progression, amount changes, or close-date slips. Deal is off track: created in Formal Evaluation stage (implying RFP already in-flight) but Zycus has not yet made first contact, obtained the RFP, or mapped any stakeholders.

items.detail: Created 28 days ago with 336-day runway to close (May 2027) but zero buyer engagement. If the RFP submission deadline is near (typical higher-ed RFP windows are 30-45 days from release) and the rep has not yet engaged the buyer or reviewed the RFP, Zycus may have already missed the window or be forced into a low-quality rushed response. Unknown RFP timeline is the single highest vulnerability.

UC system has 10 campuses with federated procurement (each campus CPO has autonomy). System-wide deals require consensus or at least non-veto from campus leaders. Zero multi-threading (no campus stakeholders mapped or engaged) means Zycus cannot navigate campus politics. A single campus veto (e.g., 'Berkeley prefers Jaggaer', 'UCSD requires ERP integration Zycus cannot deliver') can kill the deal or force a fragmented outcome (some campuses adopt Zycus, others stay on legacy). This vulnerability is acute at day 28 with no stakeholder engagement.

RFP deal with no champion: <30% win rate without an internal advocate to shape criteria, provide competitive intel, and shepherd the evaluation. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement the champion gap is acute.

UC campuses likely run Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft ERP systems (common in higher-ed). S2C platform (iContract, eProcurement) must integrate with campus ERPs for PO/invoice/supplier master data sync. Integration complexity across 10 campuses with potentially different ERP instances (or different versions of the same ERP) is high. No discovery of ERP landscape or integration requirements has occurred. This will surface as a decision criterion and implementation risk in the RFP evaluation. If Zycus cannot demonstrate ERP integration capability or if integration effort is underestimated, UC may view Zycus as higher-risk than competitors with proven higher-ed ERP connectors.

UC system has strict InfoSec and data privacy requirements (public entity, handles PII, financial data, research data subject to federal regulations like FERPA, HIPAA for UC Health, export controls for research). S2C platform will be scrutinized for data residency, encryption, access controls, SOC2/FedRAMP compliance. No discovery of InfoSec requirements or stakeholders (no InfoSec contact roles, no calls). InfoSec review will be a gate in the RFP process and can delay or kill the deal if Zycus cannot meet UC requirements or if the InfoSec review timeline is underestimated.

Unknown RFP submission deadline. Deal created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation but Zycus has not yet obtained the RFP or confirmed the submission deadline. Typical higher-ed RFP windows are 30-45 days from release, so the deadline may be imminent or already past. If Zycus has missed the window, the deal is lost. If the window is still open but closing soon, Zycus must deliver a high-quality response under time pressure with no buyer discovery or champion to guide it. This is the single highest vulnerability.

RFP deal with no champion: sub-30% win rate. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement the champion gap is acute. Without an internal advocate Zycus cannot shape RFP criteria to favor its strengths (Merlin AI, federated procurement fit), cannot get competitive intel (who else is bidding, what the eval committee values), and cannot shepherd the proposal through UC system approval gates.

Informs Zycus response strategy and demo messaging (e.g., if Q&A shows UC is concerned about ERP integration complexity, Zycus emphasizes proven Oracle/Workday connectors and campus-by-campus phased rollout).

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 May 2026, 28 days ago. Close date 28 May 2027, 336 days out. Stage Formal Evaluation (implies RFP in-flight) but LastActivityDate is null and no buyer calls exist. Next_Step dated 17 Jun (9 days ago) says rep is 'working internally' and plans to engage CPO 'shortly after that' — a rep-side intent, not buyer engagement. Zero time logged in any prior stage (no qualified_date, no submission-date series), so true time-in-stage is at least 28 days with zero buyer movement. No slip history visible (deal is only 28 days old). Pace required: for a system-wide RFP across 10 UC campuses, discovery → RFP response → evaluation → shortlist → demos → negotiation → contract in 336 days is achievable IF the RFP window is still open and Zycus enters now. However, the RFP release date and submission deadline are unknown; if the window has closed or Zycus missed it, the deal is lost. The single highest risk is that the RFP timeline has already passed Zycus by.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains completely dark 28 days after creation with zero buyer contact, no RFP timeline discovered, and no stakeholders mapped. Rep's 9-day-old intent to engage CPO has not materialized. RFP submission deadline may have already passed without Zycus participation.

flags: Zero champion at day 28 on a complex, multi-campus RFP: without an internal advocate to provide competitive intel, shape evaluation criteria, and navigate the cross-campus politics (UC has 10 campuses with federated procurement, each with a campus CPO who may have veto power or strong influence), Zycus is responding blind. RFP deals without a champion have <30% win rate. The move is to identify a champion candidate within the first two discovery calls (look for a campus or UCOP procurement leader frustrated with the status quo, excited about AI/Merlin, or who has used Zycus elsewhere) and to invest in building that relationship (exec sponsor intro from Alexa Bradley, early Merlin demo, peer reference call with another UC or CSU system that uses Zycus) before RFP submission.
best_practice_check.summary: This deal was created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation (implying an RFP already in-flight) but remains completely dark: no buyer engagement, no RFP obtained, no stakeholders mapped, no MEDDPICC coverage. The three highest-leverage win-strategy best practices for this deal are: (1) Confirm the RFP is still open and obtain it immediately (if the submission deadline has passed, the deal is lost). (2) Secure a champion in the first round of stakeholder engagement (UCOP CPO or a campus procurement leader who can shape the evaluation and provide competitive intel). (3) Multi-thread urgently across UCOP and key campuses (Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD as the largest) to build consensus and prevent a single-campus veto.

competitive_position.summary: Competition unknown. No competitor data in Salesforce (Competitors__c null, synthesis fields unread/null), zero Avoma calls, no Next_Step__c or Task mentions. For a UC system-wide S2C + AI RFP the likely competitive set includes Jaggaer (strong higher-ed CLM presence), Coupa (P2P + Workday integration), Oracle or Ariba (if UC runs those ERPs), GEP, Ivalua, and emerging AI-focused players (Zip HQ, Fairmarkit). UC may also have legacy homegrown systems. Without buyer conversation the actual shortlist and incumbent stack are unknown. The wedge (when the buyer is engaged) is Merlin AI as a negotiation and intake differentiator vs legacy platforms, unified S2C platform vs point-solution sprawl, and Zycus higher-ed references. Current threat level: cannot rank without evidence; all inferred competitors marked dormant pending discovery.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning unknown. Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake (Zycus AI offerings), but no discovery call or RFP review has occurred to assess whether UC values AI as a differentiator or whether the agentic Merlin story (negotiation agent, intake automation, contract intelligence) resonates. Higher-ed RFPs increasingly include innovation/AI as an evaluation criterion, so Merlin could be a wedge against Jaggaer (which lacks agentic AI) and Coupa (analytics-focused AI). However, without buyer engagement Zycus has not yet positioned Merlin or learned whether UC sees AI as transformational or as vendor hype. No AIS score on record.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations unknown due to zero buyer engagement. The RFP document will specify functional requirements, but Zycus has not yet obtained or reviewed it. Typical UC system expectations for a S2C platform likely include: (1) eProcurement with campus-specific catalogs and approval workflows (federated model), (2) contract lifecycle management with clause library and compliance tracking (UC handles federal research contracts, so regulatory compliance is critical), (3) supplier management and risk monitoring, (4) ERP integration (Oracle/Workday/PeopleSoft at each campus), (5) spend analytics and reporting (system-wide visibility for UCOP, campus-level detail for CPOs), (6) InfoSec and data privacy (SOC2, data residency, encryption), (7) AI/innovation (increasingly a differentiator in higher-ed RFPs), and (8) implementation support for a large, federated rollout. Zycus product scope (iContract, iManage, eProcurement, Merlin, Merlin Intake, iCompliance, ANA) suggests a full-suite S2C response, but fit cannot be assessed without the RFP criteria and a discovery call. No exposures identified yet; all positions are 'unknown' until the RFP is reviewed.

* RUC is an enterprise-wide S2C transformation across its campuses and healthcare entities, with scope spanning Spend Analytics, eSourcing, Savings Tracking, Intake & Orchestration and CLM.
* AI is in the evaluated scope: this enterprise S2C transformation explicitly includes "Intake & Orchestration" and CLM, with Salesforce noting "Strong alignment with Zycus' Spend Analysis, iSource, Merlin Intake, iContract, and Merlin AI capabilities."
* AI sits within the evaluated scope (Merlin Intake, Merlin AI) but as one element of a broad multi-module transformation rather than the stated driver; award is expected December 2026. Formal Evaluation, 20%.

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.to: Unknown RFP submission deadline. Deal created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation but Zycus has not yet obtained the RFP or confirmed the submission deadline. Typi
deltas.subject: Unknown RFP submission deadline. Deal created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation but Zycus has not yet obtained the RFP or confirmed the submission deadline. Typical higher-ed RFP windows are 30-45 days from release, so the deadline may be imminent or already past. If Zycus has missed the window, the deal is lost. If the window is still open but closing soon, Zycus must deliver a high-quality response under time pressure with no buyer discovery or champion to guide it. This is the single highest vulnerability.

deltas.to: UC system has 10 campuses with federated procurement (each campus CPO has autonomy). System-wide deals require consensus or at least non-veto from campus leader
deltas.from: UC system has 10 campuses with federated procurement (each campus has a CPO and procurement autonomy). System-wide deals require consensus or at least non-veto
deltas.subject: UC system has 10 campuses with federated procurement (each campus has a CPO and procurement autonomy). System-wide deals require consensus or at least non-veto from campus leaders. Zero multi-threading (no campus stakeholders mapped or engaged) means Zycus cannot navigate campus politics. A single campus veto (e.g., 'Berkeley prefers Jaggaer') can kill the deal or force a fragmented outcome (some campuses adopt Zycus, others stay on legacy).

deltas.to: RFP deal with no champion: sub-30% win rate. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement the champion gap is acute. Without an internal advocate Zycus cannot shape RFP
deltas.subject: RFP deal with no champion: sub-30% win rate. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement the champion gap is acute. Without an internal advocate Zycus cannot shape RFP criteria to favor its strengths (Merlin AI, federated procurement fit), cannot get competitive intel (who else is bidding, what the eval committee values), and cannot shepherd the proposal through UC system approval gates.

deltas.to: UC campuses likely run Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft ERP systems (common in higher-ed). S2C platform (iContract, eProcurement) must integrate with campus ERPs
deltas.from: UC campuses likely run Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft ERP systems (common in higher-ed). S2C platform (iContract, eProcurement) must integrate with campus ERPs
deltas.subject: UC campuses likely run Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft ERP systems (common in higher-ed). S2C platform (iContract, eProcurement) must integrate with campus ERPs for PO/invoice/supplier master data sync. Integration complexity across 10 campuses with potentially different ERP instances is high. No discovery of ERP landscape or integration requirements; this will surface as a decision criterion and implementation risk in the RFP evaluation.

deltas.to: UC system has strict InfoSec and data privacy requirements (public entity, handles PII, financial data, research data subject to federal regulations like FERPA,
deltas.from: UC system has strict InfoSec and data privacy requirements (public entity, handles PII, financial data, research data subject to federal regulations). S2C platf
deltas.subject: UC system has strict InfoSec and data privacy requirements (public entity, handles PII, financial data, research data subject to federal regulations). S2C platform will be scrutinized for data residency, encryption, access controls, SOC2/FedRAMP compliance. No discovery of InfoSec requirements or stakeholders (no InfoSec contact roles). InfoSec review will be a gate in the RFP process and can delay or kill the deal if Zycus cannot meet UC requirements.

deltas.to: RFP timeline is the single highest vulnerability: typical higher-ed RFP windows are 30-45 days from release. Deal created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation stage
deltas.subject: RFP timeline is the single highest vulnerability: typical higher-ed RFP windows are 30-45 days from release. Deal created 28 days ago in Formal Evaluation stage, but Zycus has not yet obtained the RFP or confirmed the submission deadline. If the window has closed, the deal is lost. The move is to contact the UCOP CPO or RFP coordinator immediately (by phone if necessary) to confirm the RFP is still open, request the document, and learn the submission deadline. This is a day-1 action.

deltas.to: RFP deals without a champion have sub-30% win rates. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement, Zycus has no internal advocate to shape criteria, provide competitive
deltas.subject: RFP deals without a champion have sub-30% win rates. At day 28 with zero buyer engagement, Zycus has no internal advocate to shape criteria, provide competitive intel, or shepherd the proposal through UC system politics. The move is to identify and cultivate a champion in the first round of stakeholder calls: look for a UCOP or campus leader who owns the S2C initiative, sees Zycus as the best fit, and has the credibility to influence the evaluation committee. Champion-building is the top priority after confirming the RFP timeline.

deltas.to: UC system-wide deal requires multi-campus consensus or at least non-veto from individual campuses (10 campuses with federated procurement autonomy). A single la
deltas.subject: UC system-wide deal requires multi-campus consensus or at least non-veto from individual campuses (10 campuses with federated procurement autonomy). A single large campus (e.g., Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD) preferring a different vendor (e.g., 'Berkeley wants Jaggaer') can kill the deal or force a fragmented outcome. Zero contact roles means Zycus has not yet mapped or engaged any campus stakeholders. The move is to multi-thread urgently: in parallel with the UCOP CPO engagement, reach out to the CPOs or procurement directors at the 3-4 largest/most influential campuses (Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD, Davis) to understand their priorities, build relationships, and secure their buy-in. This prevents a late-stage campus veto.

deltas.to: RFP-track deal with no RFP document or timeline: Next_Step__c (17 Jun, 9 days ago) mentions 'understand RFP requirements and deadlines' but no evidence the rep
deltas.subject: RFP-track deal with no RFP document or timeline: Next_Step__c (17 Jun, 9 days ago) mentions 'understand RFP requirements and deadlines' but no evidence the rep has secured the RFP, knows the submission deadline, or understands the evaluation criteria. Public higher-ed RFPs often have tight submission windows (30-45 days from release); if the RFP is already live and the deadline is near, Zycus may have missed the window or be forced into a rushed, low-quality response. The move is to request the RFP document and timeline from the CPO (Paul Williams or sourcing lead) within 48 hours and to escalate to the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) if the rep cannot secure it (she can leverage her UC relationships or exec-to-exec channels).

deltas.to: Zero champion at day 28 on a complex, multi-campus RFP: without an internal advocate to provide competitive intel, shape evaluation criteria, and navigate the c
deltas.subject: Zero champion at day 28 on a complex, multi-campus RFP: without an internal advocate to provide competitive intel, shape evaluation criteria, and navigate the cross-campus politics (UC has 10 campuses with federated procurement, each with a campus CPO who may have veto power or strong influence), Zycus is responding blind. RFP deals without a champion have <30% win rate. The move is to identify a champion candidate within the first two discovery calls (look for a campus or UCOP procurement leader frustrated with the status quo, excited about AI/Merlin, or who has used Zycus elsewhere) and to invest in building that relationship (exec sponsor intro from Alexa Bradley, early Merlin demo, peer reference call with another UC or CSU system that uses Zycus) before RFP submission.

deltas.to: Multi-threading gap: zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRole records and no evidence of engagement with the evaluation committee. UC system-wide deals require bu
deltas.subject: Multi-threading gap: zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRole records and no evidence of engagement with the evaluation committee. UC system-wide deals require buy-in from UCOP (system-wide procurement/IT leadership) AND campus stakeholders (campus CPOs, IT directors, legal/compliance leads). A single-threaded relationship (or zero relationships, as here) cannot navigate the federated decision process. The move is to map the evaluation committee (request the RFP to see the committee composition, or ask the CPO directly) and to conduct multi-campus discovery (at minimum: UCOP + 2-3 largest campuses like Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD) within the next 21 days to build cross-campus support and surface campus-specific concerns early.

value.how_we_win: Merlin AI negotiation + intake automation vs Jaggaer's weaker AI; unified S2C platform; higher-ed references with AI use cases

value.source: Inference: Coupa strong in P2P/spend, Workday integration, common in higher-ed
value.how_we_win: Zycus CLM + compliance depth vs Coupa's P2P-first model; Merlin AI for contract negotiation (Coupa weak here)

value.name: Incumbent homegrown or legacy system

value.how_we_win: Modern cloud platform, AI/Merlin automation, unified data vs fragmented legacy; quantify cost of maintaining homegrown

value.detail: UC system has 10 campuses with federated procurement (each campus CPO has autonomy). System-wide deals require consensus or at least non-veto from campus leaders. Zero multi-threading (no campus stakeholders mapped or engaged) means Zycus cannot navigate campus politics. A single campus veto (e.g., 'Berkeley prefers Jaggaer', 'UCSD requires ERP integration Zycus cannot deliver') can kill the deal or force a fragmented outcome (some campuses adopt Zycus, others stay on legacy). This vulnerability is acute at day 28 with no stakeholder engagement.
value.source: Zero OpportunityContactRole records; no campus-level engagement; inference from UC system federated structure

value.detail: UC campuses likely run Oracle, Workday, or PeopleSoft ERP systems (common in higher-ed). S2C platform (iContract, eProcurement) must integrate with campus ERPs for PO/invoice/supplier master data sync. Integration complexity across 10 campuses with potentially different ERP instances (or different versions of the same ERP) is high. No discovery of ERP landscape or integration requirements has occurred. This will surface as a decision criterion and implementation risk in the RFP evaluation. If Zycus cannot demonstrate ERP integration capability or if integration effort is underestimated, UC may view Zycus as higher-risk than competitors with proven higher-ed ERP connectors.
value.source: Inference from higher-ed norms; no buyer discovery of IT/ERP stack

value.detail: UC system has strict InfoSec and data privacy requirements (public entity, handles PII, financial data, research data subject to federal regulations like FERPA, HIPAA for UC Health, export controls for research). S2C platform will be scrutinized for data residency, encryption, access controls, SOC2/FedRAMP compliance. No discovery of InfoSec requirements or stakeholders (no InfoSec contact roles, no calls). InfoSec review will be a gate in the RFP process and can delay or kill the deal if Zycus cannot meet UC requirements or if the InfoSec review timeline is underestimated.
value.source: Inference from UC public-entity status and higher-ed regulatory environment; zero InfoSec stakeholder contact

value.flag: Zero champion at day 28 on a complex, multi-campus RFP: without an internal advocate to provide competitive intel, shape evaluation criteria, and navigate the cross-campus politics (UC has 10 campuses with federated procurement, each with a campus CPO who may have veto power or strong influence), Zycus is responding blind. RFP deals without a champion have <30% win rate. The move is to identify a champion candidate within the first two discovery calls (look for a campus or UCOP procurement leader frustrated with the status quo, excited about AI/Merlin, or who has used Zycus elsewhere) and to invest in building that relationship (exec sponsor intro from Alexa Bradley, early Merlin demo, peer reference call with another UC or CSU system that uses Zycus) before RFP submission.

value.flag: Multi-threading gap: zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRole records and no evidence of engagement with the evaluation committee. UC system-wide deals require buy-in from UCOP (system-wide procurement/IT leadership) AND campus stakeholders (campus CPOs, IT directors, legal/compliance leads). A single-threaded relationship (or zero relationships, as here) cannot navigate the federated decision process. The move is to map the evaluation committee (request the RFP to see the committee composition, or ask the CPO directly) and to conduct multi-campus discovery (at minimum: UCOP + 2-3 largest campuses like Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD) within the next 21 days to build cross-campus support and surface campus-specific concerns early.
